content:
# Surface Wave

**Summary**: Seismic waves that travel along the surface of the Earth.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] studied the scattering of surface waves by near-surface diffractors.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] applied SWI to refine the initial velocity model at the top 200m of the study area.

## Scientific Context

- [[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009]] Surface waves are often a source of noise in land seismic surveys.

- [[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018]] applied SWI to refine the initial velocity model at the top 200m of the study area.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods



## Key Findings Across Sources

- Surface waves can be scattered by features like karsts or artificial objects. ([[2009-al-lehyani|Al-Lehyani, 2009, p. 12]])

- SWI provided a more accurate velocity at the top 200m, leading to a dramatic improvement in RTM images. ([[2018-mohamed|Mohamed, 2018, p. 46]])
